hero.badge: Open source. Runs 100% on your machine.
hero.heading: Your AI forgets everything between sessions
hero.subtitle: Context Vault saves your decisions, patterns, and project context once, then retrieves them automatically in every future session. Works with Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, and any MCP-compatible tool.

leftPanelLines.text: Ready. Picking up where you left off.

stats.label: Setup time
stats.description: One command installs the server and connects it to your AI editor automatically.

stats.label: MCP client
stats.description: Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Zed, ChatGPT, VS Code. One vault, every tool.

stats.label: Local & yours
stats.description: Plain markdown files on your machine. No cloud, no account, no lock-in.

features.title: Never re-explain your stack
features.description: Every decision, pattern, and convention you save persists forever. Start a new session next week and your AI already knows your architecture.

features.title: Find anything, however you described it
features.description: Wrote 'SQLite' last month, search 'local database' today? It still finds it. Semantic and full-text search work together across your entire vault.

features.title: One memory across every AI tool
features.description: Same vault, every editor. Save context in Claude Code, retrieve it in Cursor. Your knowledge follows you, not the tool.

features.title: Your data stays on your machine
features.description: Plain markdown files you can read, grep, and git push. No cloud dependency, no vendor lock-in. You own every byte.

items.question: What is Context Vault?
items.answer: Context Vault is a persistent memory layer for AI coding tools. It saves your decisions, patterns, and project context as plain markdown files, then automatically retrieves them in every new AI session. Think of it as long-term memory for Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, and any MCP-compatible client.

items.question: How long does setup take?
items.answer: About 2 minutes. Run 'npx context-vault' to install and configure. It auto-detects your AI editor and sets up the MCP connection. No account, no API key, no configuration files to edit. Updates happen automatically.

items.question: Which AI tools are supported?
items.answer: Any MCP-compatible client works out of the box: Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Zed, and more. Browser-based tools like ChatGPT can connect via the REST API.

items.question: Where is my data stored?
items.answer: On your machine, in a folder you choose. Context Vault stores everything as plain markdown files with a local SQLite index for search. Nothing is sent to the cloud. We never see or access your data.

items.question: Can I move or back up my vault?
items.answer: Yes, it's just a folder of markdown files. Copy it, git push it, sync it with Dropbox, or move it to another machine. No export step, no migration tool needed.

items.question: Is Context Vault free?
items.answer: Yes, completely. Context Vault is MIT-licensed and free forever. The entire codebase is open source. You can self-host, modify, and redistribute it.

items.question: How can I contribute?
items.answer: Context Vault is built in the open on GitHub. File issues, submit pull requests, or build your own integrations. The project is actively maintained and contributions are welcome.
